prompt:
War story first: our collab-editor backend used to byte-slice user text, and the day someone pasted a 日本語 comment it panicked on a char boundary in prod. Never again — the replacement buffer speaks CHAR indices (Unicode scalar values) everywhere in its public API and converts to bytes strictly internally. That buffer is this ticket. Implement src/lib.rs of crate rs-ropelite; the acceptance tests (tests/rope.rs) exist and include a scripted differential run against a Vec<char> model, heavy on CJK/emoji/combining marks. tests/, Cargo.toml, .gitignore: hands off. Stdlib only.

Design: a rope-lite — the text lives in an ordered list of small string chunks so middle edits don't shuffle megabytes. pub const DEFAULT_CHUNK_CAPACITY: usize = 32. Structural invariant the tests check after every operation: every chunk holds between 1 and capacity CHARS (capacity counts chars, not bytes), an empty buffer has zero chunks, and the chunks concatenate to exactly the content. How you split/merge chunks is your call as long as that invariant holds after each public call.

pub struct TextBuffer (+ impl Default, and impl Display so to_string() yields the content):
- new() -> Self (default capacity), with_chunk_capacity(cap: usize) -> Self (must panic with a message containing "chunk capacity" when cap == 0), from_text(s: &str) -> Self (default capacity).
- char_len(&self) -> usize, is_empty(&self) -> bool, chunk_capacity(&self) -> usize, chunk_count(&self) -> usize, chunks(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &str> + '_.
- insert(&mut self, at: usize, text: &str) -> Result<(), EditError> — at is a char index, 0..=char_len (inserting at char_len appends). Inserting "" is a no-op but the bounds check still applies.
- delete(&mut self, start: usize, end: usize) -> Result<(), EditError> — removes the char range [start, end); delete(k, k) is a no-op.
- slice(&self, start: usize, end: usize) -> Result<String, EditError> — copies out the char range [start, end), which routinely crosses chunk seams.

pub enum EditError (derive Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq; impl Display + std::error::Error): IndexOutOfBounds { index: usize, len: usize } for insert past the end; for ranges, InvalidRange { start: usize, end: usize } when start > end — checked FIRST — and RangeOutOfBounds { start: usize, end: usize, len: usize } when end > char_len. Display messages exactly: "char index 7 out of bounds (len 3)", "invalid char range 5..2", "char range 2..9 out of bounds (len 4)". A failed operation must leave the buffer completely untouched.

The non-negotiable: correctness on multibyte text. "日本語のテキスト" is 8 chars; inserting at char 3 of it must land between 語 and の no matter how the chunks are cut; a combining mark (e\u{301}) is its own char and inserting between the base letter and the mark is legal. If any code path indexes a String with a raw byte offset that didn't come from a char-boundary computation, the differential test will catch it. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
